---
title: "AI Training Feedback Survey"
format: pdf
---

# AI Training Feedback Survey

## Section 1: Overall Experience

**1. On a scale of 1 to 5, how would you rate your overall experience with this training program?**
*(1 = Poor, 5 = Excellent)*

**2. How would you describe the overall pace of the training?**
- [ ] Much too slow
- [ ] A bit too slow
- [ ] Just right
- [ ] A bit too fast
- [ ] Much too fast

---

## Section 2: Core Comprehension & Confidence

*Please rate your current level of confidence in performing tasks related to the following modules. (1 = Not confident at all, 5 = Highly confident/Could do it independently)*

**3. Environment & Version Control:** Setting up Python/Jupyter and collaborating using Git and GitHub.
- Scale: 1 - 2 - 3 - 4 - 5

**4. Python Programming:** Using Python basics, writing functions, and applying Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) concepts.
- Scale: 1 - 2 - 3 - 4 - 5

**5. Data Analysis:** Using Pandas to clean, filter, group, and preprocess datasets.
- Scale: 1 - 2 - 3 - 4 - 5

**6. Machine Learning:** Using scikit-learn for supervised learning (regression/classification), feature engineering, and evaluating models.
- Scale: 1 - 2 - 3 - 4 - 5

**7. Deep Learning:** Building, training, and optimizing Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) using PyTorch (e.g., working with image datasets like the Cassava plant project).
- Scale: 1 - 2 - 3 - 4 - 5

**8. Agentic AI:** Understanding AI agent concepts and building agents using Opencode.
- Scale: 1 - 2 - 3 - 4 - 5

---

## Section 3: Prior Exposure to Topics

*This helps us understand if difficulty was due to prior experience or teaching style.*

**9. Before this training, how would you describe your prior experience with each of the following topics?**

| Topic | No prior experience | Some exposure (heard of it) | Used it briefly before | Regular user |
|-------|---------------------|----------------------------|-----------------------|--------------|
| Python Programming | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] |
| Git/GitHub | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] |
| Pandas/Data Analysis | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] |
| Machine Learning (scikit-learn) | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] |
| Deep Learning (PyTorch/TensorFlow) | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] |
| AI Agents/Opencode | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] | [ ] |

**10. Did you have any prior programming experience with Python before this course?**
- [ ] No programming experience at all
- [ ] A little (wrote simple scripts in Python)
- [ ] Moderate (comfortable with Python basics)
- [ ] Extensive (professional Python developer)

---

## Section 4: Post-Class Review Habits

*This helps us understand how much self-study happened after class.*

**11. After each class session, did you make time to review the materials on your own?**
- [ ] Yes, after every single class
- [ ] Yes, after most classes (4-6 out of 7)
- [ ] Yes, after some classes (2-3 out of 7)
- [ ] Rarely (1 or none)
- [ ] Never

**12. How soon after each class did you typically review the materials?**
- [ ] Same day or next day
- [ ] Within 2-3 days
- [ ] Within a week
- [ ] More than a week later
- [ ] I didn't review after class

**13. When reviewing, which of the following did you do? (Select all that apply)**
- [ ] Reran the Jupyter notebooks from start to finish
- [ ] Read through the notebooks without running code
- [ ] Tried exercises on my own without looking at solutions
- [ ] Modified the code to experiment
- [ ] Reviewed the slide decks/HTML files
- [ ] I didn't review at all

**14. Did you need to reinstall Python or any packages after the training sessions?**
- [ ] Yes, I had to reinstall everything from scratch
- [ ] Yes, but only some packages
- [ ] No, everything still worked
- [ ] I never set up my environment after the first day

**15. How would you rate the thoroughness of your post-class review?**
- [ ] Very thorough - I reviewed all materials and completed exercises
- [ ] Somewhat thorough - I reviewed most materials
- [ ] Light review - I only skimmed through
- [ ] No review at all

**16. When you reviewed, approximately how much of the materials did you cover?**
- [ ] All modules (100%)
- [ ] Most modules (75-99%)
- [ ] About half of the modules (50-74%)
- [ ] Only a few modules (25-49%)
- [ ] Very little or none (< 25%)
- [ ] I didn't review at all

---

## Section 5: Course Content Feedback

**19. Which of the modules above did you find the MOST valuable or interesting, and why?**
_____________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________

**20. Which module did you find the MOST challenging or confusing to grasp? What made it difficult?**
_____________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________

**21. How helpful were the provided Jupyter/Quarto notebooks and hands-on datasets in helping you understand the concepts?**
- [ ] Extremely helpful
- [ ] Very helpful
- [ ] Somewhat helpful
- [ ] Not very helpful

---

## Section 6: Identifying Gaps

**22. Was there any specific concept, tool, or framework you were hoping to learn in this course that we did NOT cover?**
_____________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________

**23. If we were to add an "Advanced Track" or extra modules to this training, which of the following topics would you be most eager to see? (Select up to 3)**
- [ ] Natural Language Processing (NLP) & Large Language Models (LLMs)
- [ ] MLOps & Model Deployment (e.g., Docker, AWS, Streamlit/Gradio apps)
- [ ] Advanced Computer Vision techniques (Object Detection, Segmentation)
- [ ] Time Series Forecasting
- [ ] Unsupervised Learning (Clustering, Dimensionality Reduction)
- [ ] Data Collection & Web Scraping
- [ ] Advanced Agentic AI Workflows
- [ ] Other: ______________

**24. Did you feel there was a good balance between theoretical explanations and hands-on coding?**
- [ ] Too much theory, not enough coding
- [ ] Perfect balance
- [ ] Too much coding, not enough theoretical background

---

## Section 7: Final Thoughts

**25. Now that you've completed the training, do you feel equipped to apply these AI and Data Science skills to real-world projects or your career?**
- [ ] Yes, definitely
- [ ] Yes, but I need a bit more practice
- [ ] I'm unsure where to start
- [ ] No, I still feel unprepared

**26. Any final feedback, suggestions, or comments for the instructor to improve this course for the next cohort?**
_____________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________

**27. Any feedback, suggestions, or comments for the organizers and sponsors (GIZ, CodeTrain Africa, Speedykom Group, WINS Global Consult) on how to improve the training program?**
_____________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________
